Files

rattler_repodata_gateway/fetch/cache/
mod.rs

1mod cache_headers;
2
3use std::{path::Path, str::FromStr, time::SystemTime};
4
5pub use cache_headers::CacheHeaders;
6use fs_err as fs;
7use rattler_digest::Blake2b256;
8use serde::{Deserialize, Deserializer, Serialize, Serializer};
9use url::Url;
10
11/// Representation of the `.info.json` file alongside a `repodata.json` file.
12#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
13pub struct RepoDataState {
14    /// The URL from where the repodata was downloaded. This is the URL of the
15    /// `repodata.json`, `repodata.json.zst`, or another variant. This is
16    /// different from the subdir url which does NOT include the final
17    /// filename.
18    pub url: Url,
19
20    /// The HTTP cache headers send along with the last response.
21    #[serde(flatten)]
22    pub cache_headers: CacheHeaders,
23
24    /// The timestamp of the repodata.json on disk
25    #[serde(
26        deserialize_with = "duration_from_nanos",
27        serialize_with = "duration_to_nanos",
28        rename = "mtime_ns"
29    )]
30    pub cache_last_modified: SystemTime,
31
32    /// The size of the repodata.json file on disk.
33    #[serde(rename = "size")]
34    pub cache_size: u64,
35
36    /// The blake2 hash of the file
37    #[serde(
38        default,
39        skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none",
40        deserialize_with = "deserialize_blake2_hash",
41        serialize_with = "serialize_blake2_hash"
42    )]
43    pub blake2_hash: Option<blake2::digest::Output<Blake2b256>>,
44
45    /// Whether or not zst is available for the subdirectory
46    pub has_zst: Option<Expiring<bool>>,
47
48    /// Whether a bz2 compressed version is available for the subdirectory
49    pub has_bz2: Option<Expiring<bool>>,
50}
51
52impl RepoDataState {
53    /// Reads and parses a file from disk.
54    pub fn from_path(path: &Path) -> Result<RepoDataState, std::io::Error> {
55        let content = fs::read_to_string(path)?;
56        Ok(Self::from_str(&content)?)
57    }
58
59    /// Save the cache state to the specified file.
60    pub fn to_path(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<(), std::io::Error> {
61        let file = fs::File::create(path)?;
62        Ok(serde_json::to_writer_pretty(file, self)?)
63    }
64}
65
66impl FromStr for RepoDataState {
67    type Err = serde_json::Error;
68
69    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
70        serde_json::from_str(s)
71    }
72}
73
74/// Represents a value and when the value was last checked.
75#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
76pub struct Expiring<T> {
77    pub value: T,
78
79    // #[serde(with = "chrono::serde::ts_seconds")]
80    pub last_checked: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>,
81}
82
83impl<T> Expiring<T> {
84    pub fn value(&self, expiration: chrono::Duration) -> Option<&T> {
85        if chrono::Utc::now().signed_duration_since(self.last_checked) >= expiration {
86            None
87        } else {
88            Some(&self.value)
89        }
90    }
91}
92
93/// Deserializes a [`SystemTime`] by parsing an integer and converting that as a
94/// nanosecond based unix epoch timestamp to a [`SystemTime`].
95fn duration_from_nanos<'de, D>(deserializer: D) -> Result<SystemTime, D::Error>
96where
97    D: Deserializer<'de>,
98{
99    use serde::de::Error;
100    SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH
101        .checked_add(std::time::Duration::from_nanos(Deserialize::deserialize(
102            deserializer,
103        )?))
104        .ok_or_else(|| D::Error::custom("the time cannot be represented internally"))
105}
106
107/// Serializes a [`SystemTime`] by converting it to a nanosecond based unix
108/// epoch timestamp.
109fn duration_to_nanos<S: Serializer>(time: &SystemTime, s: S) -> Result<S::Ok, S::Error> {
110    use serde::ser::Error;
111    time.duration_since(SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH)
112        .map_err(|_err| S::Error::custom("duration cannot be computed for file time"))?
113        .as_nanos()
114        .serialize(s)
115}
116
117fn deserialize_blake2_hash<'de, D>(
118    deserializer: D,
119) -> Result<Option<blake2::digest::Output<Blake2b256>>, D::Error>
120where
121    D: Deserializer<'de>,
122{
123    use serde::de::Error;
124    match Option::<&'de str>::deserialize(deserializer)? {
125        Some(str) => Ok(Some(
126            rattler_digest::parse_digest_from_hex::<Blake2b256>(str)
127                .ok_or_else(|| D::Error::custom("failed to parse blake2 hash"))?,
128        )),
129        None => Ok(None),
130    }
131}
132
133fn serialize_blake2_hash<S: Serializer>(
134    time: &Option<blake2::digest::Output<Blake2b256>>,
135    s: S,
136) -> Result<S::Ok, S::Error> {
137    match time.as_ref() {
138        None => s.serialize_none(),
139        Some(hash) => format!("{hash:x}").serialize(s),
140    }
141}
142
143#[cfg(test)]
144mod test {
145    use std::str::FromStr;
146
147    use super::RepoDataState;
148
149    const JSON_STATE_ONE: &str = r#"{
150        "cache_control": "public, max-age=1200",
151        "etag": "\"bec332621e00fc4ad87ba185171bcf46\"",
152        "has_zst": {
153            "last_checked": "2023-02-13T14:08:50Z",
154            "value": true
155        },
156        "mod": "Mon, 13 Feb 2023 13:49:56 GMT",
157        "mtime_ns": 1676297333020928000,
158        "size": 156627374,
159        "url": "https://conda.anaconda.org/conda-forge/win-64/repodata.json.zst"
160    }"#;
161
162    const JSON_STATE_TWO: &str = r#"{
163      "url": "https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/main/osx-64/repodata.json.zst",
164      "etag": "W/\"2f8b1ff101d75e40adf28c3fcbcd330b\"",
165      "mod": "Thu, 18 May 2023 13:28:44 GMT",
166      "cache_control": "public, max-age=30",
167      "mtime_ns": 1684418349941482000,
168      "size": 38001429,
169      "blake2_hash": "a1bb42ccd11d5610189380b8b0a71ca0fa7e3273ff6235ae1d543606041eb3bd",
170      "has_zst": {
171        "value": true,
172        "last_checked": "2023-05-18T13:59:07.112638Z"
173      },
174      "has_bz2": {
175        "value": true,
176        "last_checked": "2023-05-18T13:59:07.112638Z"
177      }
178    }"#;
179
180    #[test]
181    pub fn test_parse_repo_data_state_one() {
182        insta::assert_yaml_snapshot!(RepoDataState::from_str(JSON_STATE_ONE).unwrap());
183    }
184
185    #[test]
186    pub fn test_parse_repo_data_state_two() {
187        insta::assert_yaml_snapshot!(RepoDataState::from_str(JSON_STATE_TWO).unwrap());
188    }
189}
